figures.caption: FIG. 8. (Color online) The specific heat CV (left panels) and squared speed of sound c2s (right panels) as functions of temperature T for different chemical potential configurations. Top panels: µQ = µS = 0; Middle panels: µB = µS = 0; Bottom panels: µB = µQ = 0.
FIG. 7. (Color online) Same as in fig. 6.

FIG. 1. (Color online) The input layer comprises four dimensions, specifically T , µB , µQ and µS . The three mass models present u/d, s and gluon, respectively. Each mass model is characterized by a neural network with identical architecture, where the corresponding network parameters are denoted as θ1, θ2 and θ3. The output will undergo numerical integration to compute the partition function, lnZ, followed by automatic differentiation to derive the corresponding thermodynamic quantities.

FIG. 2. (Color online) The baryon-strangeness correlation coefficient CBS is presented as functions of √ sNN and µB for different centrality classes. The left panel displays CBS as a function of √ sNN for 0-5% centrality, with comparisons to the LQCD calculations, HRG model, QM-HRG model and preliminary STAR results [21, 55, 60]. The red data points correspond to STAR experimental measurements, the yellow band denotes LQCD results, the black dotted line represents HRG model outcomes, and the dashed line indicates QMHRG model predictions. The color bands show the results from DLQPM. The right panel presents CBS as a function of baryon chemical potential (µB) for two centrality classes: 0-5% (most central) and 60-80% (peripheral, shown in blue and pink).
FIG. 9. (Color online) The pressure p/T 4 across the two-dimensional planes of (T , µB), (T , µS), and (T , µQ) compares with NEoS results [25].

FIG. 6. (Color online) The temperature-dependent susceptibilities χB,Q,S i,j,k are compared with LQCD results and 2D DLQPM calculations [26, 35]. The comparison includes three distinct calculations: (1) LQCD results shown as orange band, (2) 4D DLQPM calculations represented by a red dashed line, and (3) 2D DLQPM calculations indicated by a cyan dotted line.
FIG. 4. (Color online) The p/T 4, ϵ/T 4 and s/T 3 across the T - µB , T - µS and T - µQ planes.

FIG. 3. (Color online) The pressure p/T 4, energy density ε/T 4 and entropy density s/T 3 as functions of T . The solid lines represent the computational results of our model, while the bands denote the calculation results from T ′-expansion[26], with different colors corresponding to distinct values of µ/T .
